subject: chem_sl
note: 知识点分组由 Claude 依大纲起草（v1 draft），与官方 guide 措辞非逐字对应；发现偏差随时修。covered=false 表示课内还没学到。

topics.name: Structure 1: Models of the particulate nature of matter

subtopics.name: Introduction to the particulate nature of matter

kps.name: Elements, compounds and mixtures; separation techniques
Kinetic molecular theory; states of matter and changes of state
Temperature in kelvin; average kinetic energy of particles
Subatomic particles, nuclear symbols and isotopes
Mass spectra; relative atomic mass from isotopic abundance
Emission spectra; discrete energy levels of hydrogen
Sublevels and orbitals; s/p/d/f shapes and capacity
Full and condensed electron configurations; orbital diagrams (Aufbau, Hund, Pauli)

subtopics.name: Counting particles by mass: the mole

kps.name: The mole and Avogadro constant; particle–amount conversions
Molar mass; empirical and molecular formulas; percentage composition
Solution concentration (mol dm⁻³, g dm⁻³); dilution and standard solutions
Ideal gas model and its assumptions; limitations (real gases)
Molar volume; gas law calculations (PV=nRT and combined law)

topics.name: Structure 2: Models of bonding and structure

kps.name: Ion formation; formulas of ionic compounds incl. polyatomic ions
Ionic lattice and properties (melting point, conductivity, solubility)
Covalent bonds and Lewis formulas; octet rule; coordination bonds
VSEPR: electron domains, molecular shapes and bond angles
Electronegativity, bond polarity and molecular polarity
Intermolecular forces (LDF, dipole–dipole, hydrogen bonding) and physical properties
Covalent network structures (diamond, graphite, silicon dioxide)
Metallic bonding; delocalized electrons and metallic properties
Bonding triangle; classifying materials by bonding type
Alloys; addition polymers from monomers

subtopics.name: The periodic table: classification of elements

kps.name: Periodic table structure: groups, periods, blocks; electron configuration link
Periodicity: atomic radius, ionization energy, electron affinity, electronegativity
Group 1 and group 17 trends and reactions
Metal/non-metal oxides and acid–base character trend

subtopics.name: Functional groups: classification of organic compounds

kps.name: Homologous series and general formulas
Functional group identification; IUPAC nomenclature
Structural formulas (full, condensed, skeletal); structural isomers
Primary, secondary, tertiary classification (carbon/amines)
Endothermic/exothermic reactions; ΔH sign and energy level diagrams
Calorimetry: q = mcΔT and experimental ΔH determination
Bond enthalpies; ΔH from average bond enthalpy data
Hess's law and energy cycles; using ΔHf / ΔHc data
Complete vs incomplete combustion; combustion equations
Fossil fuels vs biofuels; specific energy and CO₂ considerations

topics.name: Reactivity 2: How much, how fast and how far?

subtopics.name: How much? The amount of chemical change

kps.name: Balancing equations with state symbols; mole ratio calculations
Stoichiometry across mass/volume/concentration incl. titration calculations
Limiting reactant; theoretical, experimental and percentage yield
Atom economy

subtopics.name: How fast? The rate of chemical change

kps.name: Rate definition; determining rate from concentration–time data/graphs
Collision theory; factors affecting rate (concentration, T, surface area, catalyst)
Maxwell–Boltzmann distribution and activation energy (qualitative)

subtopics.name: How far? The extent of chemical change

kps.name: Dynamic equilibrium and its characteristics
Equilibrium constant K expression; magnitude interpretation; Q vs K
Le Châtelier's principle: effects of concentration, pressure, temperature, catalyst

topics.name: Reactivity 3: What are the mechanisms of chemical change?

kps.name: Brønsted–Lowry acids/bases; conjugate acid–base pairs; amphiprotic species
pH scale, [H⁺] calculations; ion product of water Kw
Strong vs weak acids/bases; experimental distinction
Acid reactions (metals, carbonates, hydroxides); neutralization and pH curves (strong–strong)
Oxidation states; identifying redox reactions and oxidizing/reducing agents
Half-equations; balancing simple redox equations
Relative reactivity of metals; displacement reactions
Primary (voltaic) cells and electrolytic cells (molten electrolytes)
Radicals and homolytic fission
Radical substitution of alkanes with halogens (initiation/propagation/termination)

subtopics.name: Electron-pair sharing reactions: nucleophiles and electrophiles

kps.name: Nucleophiles and electrophiles; heterolytic fission
Nucleophilic substitution of halogenoalkanes (SL treatment)
Electrophilic addition to alkenes (SL treatment)
